English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell Campus Club records
Cornell Campus Club
11-8-390
2.3 cubic feet. 2.3 cubic feet.
1900-2015. Organizational records, printed material, and correspondence. Secretary's and treasurer's reports, minutes of the executive board and various committees and annual meetings, constitutions, lists of officers, newsletters and brochures, samples of printed forms, reports of activity groups, social reports, newcomers' items, history of the Campus Club, and miscellaneous items. Collection material in English 2089301
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
June 2016
EAD encoding:
Marcie Farwell, June 2016
Date modified:
Kristen Reichenbach, January 2019
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
Cornell women's organization, formed in 1900 to "welcome newcomers to the campus and to promote friendship among the women at Cornell."
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Secretary's and treasurer's reports, minutes of the executive board and various committees and annual meetings, constitutions, lists of officers, newsletters and brochures, samples of printed forms, reports of activity groups (1936-1941), social reports, newcomers' items (1949-1953), history of the Campus Club, and miscellaneous items. Includes correspondence of Mary H. Donlon, Emily Day, and Helen A. Newman. Also includes a VHS videotape made by the Quilting Group of the Campus Club in 1995 of the presentation of a quilt to Frank and Rosa Rhodes.
RELATED MATERIALS
This collection includes collection #11-8-99.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Cornell Campus Club Annual Reports
Series II. Assorted Campus Club Records
Cite As:
Cornell Campus Club Records, #11-8-390. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Women -- New York (State) -- Ithaca -- Societies and clubs.
